meta_title: Roofing in Lantana, FL | Premier Impact Windows
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs hurricane-rated roofing in Lantana, FL. CertainTeed shingles & West Lake tile. Licensed, in-house crews. Free estimates. From $87/mo.
h1: Lantana's Most Trusted Hurricane-Rated Roofing Replacement & Repair
intro:
Lantana, Florida sits just 1.5 miles from the Atlantic Ocean — close enough that every tropical system churning up the coastline makes itself known in neighborhoods like Pine Ridge, South Shores, and Lantana Homes. That proximity to open water means sustained wind loads, relentless salt air exposure, and the kind of driving rain that exposes every weakness in an aging roof system. For homeowners in the 33462 ZIP code, a properly installed, hurricane-rated roof isn't a luxury upgrade — it's the single most important investment protecting a home valued at an average of $350,000 or more.

With roughly 55% of Lantana's housing stock built before 1994, a significant portion of local roofs predate the modern Florida Building Code standards that were dramatically strengthened after Hurricane Andrew. Many of these older systems — whether original three-tab shingles or clay tile set without modern adhesive requirements — were not designed to withstand the 170 mph wind zone designations that Palm Beach County now enforces. If your home was built before the mid-1990s and has never had a full roof replacement, there's a strong likelihood your current system is both structurally undersized and performing well below modern energy and weather-resistance standards.

At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we've helped hundreds of South Florida homeowners replace outdated roofing systems with products built specifically for coastal high-wind environments. Our team understands the unique demands placed on roofs in Lantana — from the salt-laden breezes rolling in off Lantana Beach to the standing water that accumulates in low-lying sections near Sportsman's Park after a heavy storm. Whether you're looking for a full architectural shingle replacement or a clay tile system that complements the character of your neighborhood, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing brings the experience, the product selection, and the in-house craftsmanship to get it done right the first time.

section_why.h2: Why Lantana Homeowners Choose Premier Impact Windows & Roofing for Roof Replacement
section_why.body:
Choosing a roofing contractor in South Florida is a decision that carries serious long-term consequences. Unfortunately, the region is well known for storm-chasing contractors who arrive after a hurricane, offer quick patch jobs, and disappear before the next rainy season when the problems resurface. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing operates differently. We are a fully licensed and insured company with an A+ BBB rating, and every single installation is completed by our own in-house crews — never subcontracted. That distinction matters enormously when it comes to accountability, quality control, and long-term warranty coverage.

Our team brings over 60 years of combined experience installing roofing systems across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties. We've worked on everything from modest mid-century ranch homes in Lantana Homes to larger coastal properties in South Shores and Pine Ridge. That local experience means we understand soil conditions, slope variations, HOA requirements, and the specific wear patterns that coastal proximity creates for different roofing materials. When we assess your roof, we're not running a generic checklist — we're evaluating your specific home against conditions unique to the 33462 corridor.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire process from start to finish. We pull permits directly through the Town of Lantana Building Department, coordinate all required inspections, and ensure every installation meets or exceeds current Florida Building Code requirements for Palm Beach County. Our clients don't have to chase down a permit or wonder whether the work was inspected — we manage every step so homeowners can focus on what matters. With more than 500 homes completed across the tri-county area, we have a documented track record that speaks louder than any sales pitch.

We also believe that a quality roof should be accessible to every homeowner, not just those who can pay cash upfront. That's why we offer flexible financing options starting at just $87 per month with $0 down programs available for qualified homeowners. From the first call to the final inspection, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing earns its reputation as the Kings of Service — one roof at a time.

section_history.h2: Lantana's Storm History Proves Why Your Roof Cannot Afford to Wait
section_history.body:
Lantana has been tested by major storms at least eight times since 1990, and the evidence is visible in neighborhoods across the town. Hurricane Frances swept through Palm Beach County in September 2004, delivering sustained winds and hours of torrential rain that exposed roof decks across the 33462 area. Just one year later, Hurricane Wilma — one of the most intense Atlantic hurricanes ever recorded — made a direct landfall crossing South Florida, generating catastrophic wind damage that stripped shingles, cracked tile, and overwhelmed roofing systems that had already been weakened by Frances. Homes along South Shores and the streets closest to the Intracoastal took some of the hardest hits during that back-to-back storm sequence.

Hurricane Irma in September 2017 was a stark reminder that the threat doesn't diminish with time. As Irma tracked up the western coast of Florida, its eastern rain bands hammered Palm Beach County with sustained tropical-force winds for hours. In Lantana, older pre-1994 roofs that had survived previous storms finally gave out, with widespread shingle blow-offs, lifted tile sections, and water intrusion that led to costly interior damage. Insurance claims surged throughout the ZIP code, and homeowners who had delayed roof replacements found themselves facing not just roofing bills but interior repairs to ceilings, insulation, and drywall.

The pattern is consistent and clear: Lantana's coastal position and aging housing stock create a compounding vulnerability with every storm season. A roof that survived Wilma may not survive the next major hurricane. With Atlantic storm seasons increasingly active and the town sitting squarely in a 170 mph design wind speed zone, the question isn't whether Lantana will be impacted again — it's whether your roof will be ready when it happens.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code Requirements for Roofing in Lantana and Palm Beach County
section_code.body:
While Lantana is not located within the High-Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) — a designation that applies specifically to Miami-Dade and Broward counties — the town is still subject to rigorous Florida Building Code requirements that reflect its coastal exposure and 170 mph design wind speed classification. Palm Beach County falls under the 7th Edition Florida Building Code with specific provisions for wind-borne debris regions and high-wind roofing assemblies. This means that any full roof replacement in Lantana must use products that meet or exceed the wind resistance and installation standards defined for this wind exposure category.

For shingle roofing, this includes requirements for enhanced fastening patterns — typically six nails per shingle rather than the standard four — as well as sealed roof deck requirements using approved self-adhering underlayments or secondary water barriers. For tile systems, Florida Building Code mandates specific mortar or adhesive application methods designed to prevent tile displacement under high-wind conditions. These are not optional enhancements; they are code-required minimums that every permitted job must satisfy and that Town of Lantana Building Department inspectors will verify during final inspection.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing pulls all required permits through the Town of Lantana Building Department for every project we undertake. We do not perform unpermitted work, and we never ask homeowners to sign off on work that hasn't passed inspection. Our permit process includes pre-installation review, mid-job inspections where required, and a final sign-off confirming the completed system meets current code. This documentation is not just a legal requirement — it's a critical piece of your property record that supports insurance claims, wind mitigation credits, and future resale. Working with a contractor who skips the permit process in Lantana can result in fines, forced removal of completed work, and denied insurance claims.

section_products.h2: CertainTeed and West Lake: Premium Roofing Products Built for Lantana's Coastal Climate
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs two industry-leading roofing product lines specifically selected for their performance in South Florida's demanding coastal environment. For homeowners seeking an architectural shingle solution, we exclusively install CertainTeed's Landmark Series — one of the most recognized names in high-performance residential roofing. CertainTeed Landmark shingles are manufactured with a dual-layered construction that provides superior wind resistance, and they carry a Class A fire rating along with a limited lifetime warranty. The Landmark Series is engineered to withstand wind speeds up to 110 mph in standard installation and up to 130 mph with CertainTeed's enhanced fastening specifications — making them an excellent choice for Lantana homes that want proven protection without the weight of a tile system.

For homeowners in Lantana who prefer the aesthetic warmth and long-term durability of tile roofing, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs West Lake concrete and clay tile systems. West Lake products are designed specifically for Florida's climate, offering superior resistance to UV degradation, salt air corrosion, and moisture infiltration — all critical performance factors for homes within 1.5 miles of the Atlantic coast. Concrete tile from West Lake provides exceptional mass and interlock characteristics that resist wind uplift, while their clay tile offerings deliver the Old Florida aesthetic seen throughout South Shores and older sections of Lantana Homes with the structural integrity required under current Palm Beach County code.

Both product lines are available in a wide range of colors and profiles, allowing homeowners to match their existing architecture or pursue a complete aesthetic transformation. Whether you're replacing a deteriorated shingle roof on a 1980s ranch or restoring the tile system on a mid-century Florida home, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing will help you select the right product for your structure, your budget, and your long-term protection goals.

section_cost.h2: What Does a New Roof Cost in Lantana, FL? Investment, Financing, and Real Returns
section_cost.body:
For Lantana homeowners with properties averaging $350,000 in value, a full roof replacement represents one of the highest-return investments available in home improvement. Industry data consistently shows that a new roof recovers 60–70% of its cost in immediate home value appreciation — and in South Florida's insurance-sensitive market, the financial benefits extend well beyond curb appeal. A properly permitted, hurricane-rated roof can unlock significant homeowners insurance discounts and, in many cases, pay for itself in premium savings within a decade.

Typical roof replacement costs in the Lantana area range from $12,000 to $22,000 depending on the size of the home, the pitch of the roof, the material selected, and the condition of the existing deck. CertainTeed Landmark architectural shingle systems generally fall in the mid-range, while West Lake tile systems — both concrete and clay — sit at the higher end due to material costs and the labor intensity of proper tile installation. Every Premier Impact Windows & Roofing estimate is itemized and transparent, so homeowners understand exactly what they're paying for before any work begins.

For homeowners who prefer not to pay the full cost upfront, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers flexible financing options starting at just $87 per month with $0 down programs available to qualified applicants. Our financing partners work specifically with home improvement projects, offering competitive rates and terms designed to make a quality roof accessible without draining savings or delaying a critical replacement. Given that storm damage to an unprotected roof can result in tens of thousands of dollars in interior repairs, the cost of financing a quality system today is almost always far less than the cost of deferring the decision until after the next major storm.

section_insurance.h2: How a New Roof in Lantana Can Lower Your Homeowners Insurance Premiums
section_insurance.body:
Florida homeowners insurance is among the most expensive in the nation, and Palm Beach County policyholders feel that reality acutely. Lantana's coastal position, combined with the high percentage of pre-1994 homes in the area, means many local homeowners are paying elevated premiums that reflect the perceived risk of older, under-rated roofing systems. The good news is that a properly installed, hurricane-rated roof — particularly one that meets or exceeds Florida Building Code wind resistance requirements — can directly and significantly reduce those premiums through wind mitigation credits.

Florida law requires insurance carriers to offer premium discounts to homeowners who can demonstrate specific wind-resistant features on their properties. A wind mitigation inspection, completed after a new roof installation, evaluates factors including roof shape, deck attachment, roof-to-wall connection, and opening protection. A new roof installed with code-compliant enhanced fastening, secondary water barriers, and approved underlayments routinely results in wind mitigation credits that reduce annual homeowners insurance premiums by 20–40% in Palm Beach County. For a homeowner currently paying $4,000 to $6,000 per year in insurance — which is common in the 33462 area — those savings can represent $800 to $2,400 annually.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing coordinates with independent wind mitigation inspectors and provides all required installation documentation to support your insurance discount filing. We also help homeowners navigate the re-roofing and insurance replacement process when storm damage is involved. Major carriers active in the Lantana market — including Citizens, Universal, and Heritage — all recognize wind mitigation credits under Florida's statutory framework. Our team can help you understand what documentation your insurer requires and ensure your new roof installation is positioned to deliver the maximum available discount from day one of coverage.

faqs.q: How do I know if my Lantana home needs a full roof replacement or just repairs?
faqs.a: If your roof is over 15 years old, shows widespread granule loss, has multiple areas of lifted or missing shingles or tile, or has experienced interior water intrusion in more than one location, a full replacement is likely the more cost-effective path. Patchwork repairs on aging roofs in Lantana's coastal environment tend to fail quickly due to ongoing UV and salt air degradation. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers free inspections and will provide an honest recommendation based on the actual condition of your roof — never a one-size-fits-all answer.

faqs.q: What does roofing financing look like, and can I really get $0 down?
faqs.a: Yes — Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing programs starting at $87 per month with $0 down options available for qualified applicants. Our financing is provided through home improvement lending partners who specialize in roofing and remodeling projects. Terms and rates vary by creditworthiness, but many Lantana homeowners are surprised by how affordable monthly payments are relative to the long-term value and insurance savings a new roof delivers. We'll walk you through financing options at no obligation during your free estimate.

faqs.q: Do I need a permit to replace my roof in Lantana, and how does that process work?
faqs.a: Yes, a permit is required for all full roof replacements in Lantana. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permitting process through the Town of Lantana Building Department on your behalf. We submit all required documentation, coordinate inspections at each required phase, and obtain final sign-off before the project is considered complete. You'll receive a copy of the closed permit for your records — a critical document for insurance wind mitigation filings and future property sales.

faqs.q: How long does a roof replacement take in Lantana?
faqs.a: Most full roof replacements on Lantana residential homes are completed within one to three days, depending on the size of the home, the roofing material, and weather conditions. Tile roofs typically take longer than shingle systems due to the precision required in layout and fastening. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing schedules work to avoid forecasted rain windows whenever possible, and our in-house crews work efficiently without sacrificing the quality of installation. We'll give you a realistic timeline during your estimate consultation.

faqs.q: Will a new roof actually lower my homeowners insurance in Palm Beach County?
faqs.a: In most cases, yes — significantly. A new hurricane-rated roof that meets Florida Building Code requirements qualifies for wind mitigation credits under Florida law, which all licensed carriers must apply to your premium. Lantana homeowners who complete a post-installation wind mitigation inspection commonly see insurance premium reductions of 20–40% annually. On a $5,000/year policy, that's $1,000–$2,000 in annual savings. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing provides all documentation needed to support your wind mitigation filing and can refer you to a qualified inspector.

cta_headline: Ready to Protect Your Lantana Home with a Hurricane-Rated Roof?
cta_body: Contact Premier Impact Windows & Roofing today for a free, no-obligation roof inspection and estimate — our in-house team is ready to serve Lantana homeowners with the craftsmanship and code compliance your home deserves.
